approval_scope: Approval defaults operate below AGENTS.MD invariants and only after local-first, exact-absence instruction resolution succeeds.

description: Read-only context gathering does not require a separate approval beyond the developer request.
Proposal writing is read-only and may continue inside the current task scope when no stop condition applies.
The current developer-requested task scope authorizes planned document synchronization without a separate phase prompt.
The current task scope authorizes scoped runtime implementation; destructive work and scope expansion remain separate stop conditions.
Focused and medium checks may run when required by the changed scope.
Required proof and normal Git integration may proceed when task scope and saved action settings allow; failed CI and unsafe operations still stop.
Eligible merge, main CI monitoring, and sync follow task scope and Settings; destructive cleanup always remains approval-bound.

approval_scope: For an externally activated headless repository, L1-L4 add no phase approval inside the original clear task scope, L5 adds one post-plan implementation approval, and destructive or authority-expanding actions remain separately approval-bound; the paused Control Center does not add or remove authority.

Normal isolated execution inside the current task scope requires no additional phase approval after candidate-bound Production Activation, but missing isolation, invalid launcher lineage, stale authority, credential delivery, or destructive cleanup cannot be waived.

A normal authorized release may advance a different candidate through the fresh complete Activation chain without another phase approval, but no approval preset may permit same-candidate rewind, rolled-back restart, transition skipping, prior-record deletion, or fallback to stale enforced authority.

Private complete non-executed provider descriptors need no separate approval inside isolated bootstrap, launcher-integrity, injected discovery, or development-selection regression tests, but they grant no provider, cost, launch, release, or Activation authority and cannot be selected for real work; the live default provider probe requires a read-only host root and private-tmpfs executable binding, while missing containment keeps Production blocked and permits only explicit positive-case skips plus guided refusal.

Creating the ignored checkout identity during an already authorized synchronized-main Controller installation is normal setup, but missing or invalid identity stops rather than prompting for a substitute ID.
